zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Clarke於1500年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾諾斯 韋斯頓時，她的父親Sir James Clarke15歲，母親Elizabeth Ashby19歲。她於1524年與William Curtis在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1563年11月在英國英格蘭艾可享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特斯萊特。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Clarke was born in 1500, in North Weston, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir James Clarke, was 15 and her mother, Elizabeth Ashby, was 19. She married William Curtis in 1524, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in November 1563, in Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Chislet, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Curtis в 1524 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла ноябрь 1563 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности числет, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
